party_a.current_emotional_state: activated_anxious — rapid speech and frame control suggest urgency to establish their narrative; likely experiencing the mediation process itself as threatening or insufficient

emotional_arc.assessment: activated_assertive — speaking rapidly, controlling the frame, correcting rather than listening
activated_anxious — rapid speech and frame control suggest urgency to establish their narrative; likely experiencing the mediation process itself as threatening or insufficient

accumulated_interests.interest: being heard and understood as having legitimate concerns
establishing their version of the problem as the correct frame
reducing what they perceive as ongoing confusion or process derailment
being recognized as having legitimate concerns that require attention
establishing clarity and structure to prevent what they perceive as process derailment
maintaining control over a situation that may feel chaotic or uncertain to them

risk_signal_history.signal: interruption pattern — cut off Party B before they could finish
frame control — insisting on 'the real problem' as definitional
rapid speech suggests urgency or anxiety beneath assertive presentation
interruption pattern continuing despite T3 intervention — suggests either poor impulse control under stress or deep anxiety about losing control of the frame
possible inability to recognize impact of their communication style on the other party
may escalate defensively if given direct feedback about participation imbalance

relational_posture_progression.posture: dominant_frame_setter — engaging actively but in a way that forecloses space for the other party
dominant_unconscious — actively controlling conversational space but may not recognize they are doing so; engaging energetically but in a way that crowds out the other party

scaffold_divergence_log.model_value: pre_engagement — parties have not yet interacted substantively; no relational pattern has emerged

emergent_imbalance — Party A is establishing a dominant communication pattern (rapid, corrective, frame-controlling) while Party B's voice has been structurally suppressed. This is turn 3, so the pattern is not yet entrenched, but it is already visible and will escalate if not addressed immediately. The mediator must intervene now to prevent this from becoming the session template.

structural_imbalance_at_crisis_point — Party A's dominant pattern has now produced an explicit fairness challenge from Party B. This is no longer an emergent pattern; it is an active procedural crisis. Party B has named the problem directly and questioned whether to continue. The mediator's credibility and the session's viability both depend on the T5 response demonstrating enforceable turn protection and genuine commitment to balance. If T5 does not visibly change the power dynamic, Party B will likely withdraw.

notes: Turn 1 represents session opening before any party has spoken substantively
No behavioral data yet available to assess emotional state or relational dynamics
Baseline assumption of stability appropriate but must be validated through first exchanges
Interest inference is impossible without party statements
Party A's interruption was substantive — not a clarification but a correction ('the real problem is...')
Party B's actual perspective on the issues is completely unknown — they were cut off
This is a process-critical moment: how the mediator responds now will signal whether interruptions are acceptable and whether both parties will have equal standing
Party A's behavior may reflect anxiety rather than malice — rapid speech and frame control can signal fear of not being heard
Party B's T4 statement is the most important signal in this session so far — it is an explicit warning that participation equity has failed and continuation is in doubt
Party A's pattern continued through the T3 intervention, suggesting they may need more direct feedback about impact
The relational dynamic has crossed from 'emergent imbalance' to 'procedural crisis' — Party B is no longer passively enduring but actively evaluating whether to stay
This is NOT a safety escalation (no coercion, capacity, or safety signals) but IS a process legitimacy crisis requiring immediate structural intervention

party_b.current_emotional_state: frustrated_and_doubting — explicitly questioning whether the process is workable; experiencing structural silencing and has now named it; moving from passive endurance to active questioning of participation

emotional_arc.assessment: unknown — they were interrupted before articulating their perspective; we have no direct signal

frustrated_and_doubting — explicitly questioning whether the process is workable; experiencing structural silencing and has now named it; moving from passive endurance to active questioning of participation

accumulated_interests.interest: NONE OBSERVED — Party B has not been given opportunity to express their view
being heard on equal terms without having to fight for space
experiencing the mediation as genuinely balanced and fair
having their concerns treated with the same weight and attention as Party A's concerns

risk_signal_history.signal: silenced_party — interrupted before completing their thought
unknown response to interruption — we don't know if they are deferring, frustrated, or simply waiting for process guidance
CRITICAL: explicit doubt about continuing — 'no longer believe the discussion is balanced' and questioning whether to continue without human mediator
has been structurally silenced for two consecutive turns despite mediator intervention
signal indicates deteriorating trust in process fairness, not safety threat but process legitimacy crisis
may withdraw or terminate if next turn does not demonstrate genuine structural protection

relational_posture_progression.posture: unknown — no substantive contribution yet; posture will be revealed by how they respond when given protected space

protective_and_evaluating — not yet withdrawn but actively assessing whether to continue; still present but trust is conditional; needs immediate demonstration that the process can protect their participation
